input_row.title: Ensure that RDP access from the Internet is evaluated and restricted
input_row.description: Network security groups should be periodically evaluated for port misconfigurations. Where RDP is not explicitly required and narrowly configured for resources attached to a network security group, Internet-level access to Azure resources should be restricted or eliminated.
input_row.rationale: The potential security problem with using RDP over the Internet is that attackers can use various brute force techniques to gain access to Azure Virtual Machines. Once the attackers gain access, they can use a virtual machine as a launch point for compromising other machines on an Azure Virtual Network or even attack networked devices outside of Azure.
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. Go to Network security groups. 2. Under Settings, click Inbound security rules. 3. Ensure that no inbound security rule exists that matches the following: o Port: 3389 or range including 3389 o Protocol: TCP or Any o Source: 0.0.0.0/0, Internet, or Any o Action: Allow 4. Repeat steps 1-3 for each network security group. To audit from Azure Resource Graph: 1. Go to Resource Graph Explorer. 2. Click New query. 3. Paste the following into the query window: [next page] resources | where type =~ "microsoft.network/networksecuritygroups" | project id, name, securityRule = properties.securityRules | mv-expand securityRule | extend access = securityRule.properties.access, direction = securityRule.properties.direction, protocol = securityRule.properties.protocol, destinationPort = case(isempty(securityRule.properties.destinationPortRange), securityRule.properties.destinationPortRanges, securityRule.properties.destinationPortRange), sourceAddress = case(isempty(securityRule.properties.sourceAddressPrefix), securityRule.properties.sourceAddressPrefixes, securityRule.properties.sourceAddressPrefix) | where access =~ "Allow" and direction =~ "Inbound" and protocol in~ ("tcp", "") | mv-expand destinationPort | mv-expand sourceAddress | extend destinationPortMin = toint(split(destinationPort, "-")[0]), destinationPortMax = toint(split(destinationPort, "-")[-1]) | where (destinationPortMin <= 3389 and destinationPortMax >= 3389) or destinationPort == "" | where sourceAddress in~ ("*", "0.0.0.0", "internet", "any") or sourceAddress endswith "/0" 4. Click Run query. 5. Ensure that no results are returned. Audit from Azure CLI List network security groups with non-default security rules: az network nsg list --query [*].[name,securityRules] Ensure that no network security group has an inbound security rule that matches the following: "access" : "Allow" "destinationPortRange" : "3389", "*", or "<range-including-3389>" "direction" : "Inbound" "protocol" : "TCP" or "*" "sourceAddressPrefix" : "0.0.0.0/0", "Internet", or "*" Audit from Azure Policy If referencing a digital copy of this Benchmark, clicking a Policy ID will open a link to the associated Policy definition in Azure. If referencing a printed copy, you can search Policy IDs from this URL: https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Policy/PolicyMenuBlade/~/Definitions • Policy ID: 22730e10-96f6-4aac-ad84-9383d35b5917 - Name: 'Management ports should be closed on your virtual machines'
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Go to Network security groups. 2. Under Settings, click Inbound security rules. 3. Check the box next to any inbound security rule matching: o Port: 3389 or range including 3389 o Protocol: TCP or Any o Source: 0.0.0.0/0, Internet, or Any o Action: Allow 4. Click Delete. 5. Click Yes. Remediate from Azure CLI For each network security group rule requiring remediation, run the following command to delete a rule: az network nsg rule delete --resource-group <resource-group> --nsg-name <network-security-group> --name <rule> Default Value: By default, RDP access from internet is not enabled. References: 1. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security/azure-security-network-security- best-practices#disable-rdpssh-access-to-azure-virtual-machines 2. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/mcsb-network- security#ns-1-establish-network-segmentation-boundaries 3. Express Route: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/expressroute/ 4. Site-to-Site VPN: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/vpn-gateway/vpn- gateway-howto-site-to-site-resource-manager-portal 5. Point-to-Site VPN: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/vpn-gateway/vpn- gateway-howto-point-to-site-resource-manager-portal
input_row.reference: 1. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security/azure-security-network-security- best-practices#disable-rdpssh-access-to-azure-virtual-machines 2. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/mcsb-network- security#ns-1-establish-network-segmentation-boundaries 3. Express Route: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/expressroute/ 4. Site-to-Site VPN: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/vpn-gateway/vpn- gateway-howto-site-to-site-resource-manager-portal 5. Point-to-Site VPN: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/vpn-gateway/vpn- gateway-howto-point-to-site-resource-manager-portal

gpt_response:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The audit approach is determined to be Automated due to the following criteria met:
- Azure Resource Manager APIs are capable of querying network security group configurations.
- The audit instructions explicitly mention API/CLI commands for evaluation, indicating these configurations can be checked programmatically.
- The control pertains to network access rules, which are standard properties exposed by Azure APIs.

**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_network_nsg_rdp_access_restricted

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
The automated program will check the following:

- **API/CLI Commands to Use**: 
  - Azure CLI command to list network security groups and their rules:
    ```
    az network nsg list --query "[].{name:name, securityRules:securityRules}" --output json
    ```

- **Specific Resources to Enumerate**: 
  - Network Security Groups (NSGs) across all subscriptions/resource groups.

- **Exact Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate**:
  - For each NSG, check the properties of the inbound security rules:
    - `access`: must not be `"Allow"`
    - `destinationPortRange`: must not include `3389` or ranges that include `3389`
    - `protocol`: must be `"TCP"` or `"Any"`
    - `sourceAddressPrefix`: must not include `0.0.0.0/0`, `"Internet"`, or `"*"`

- **Pass/Fail Criteria**:
  - **PASS**: If no inbound security rule exists in any NSG that allows access from addresses matching the criteria above.
  - **FAIL**: If any inbound security rule exists that allows RDP access (port `3389` from any source).

- **Example API Endpoint or CLI Command**: 
  The following Azure CLI command can be run after retrieving the NSG rules to check specific rules:
  ```
  az network nsg rule list --resource-group <resource-group> --nsg-name <network-security-group> --query "[?access=='Allow' && destinationPortRange=='3389' || destinationPortRange=='*' || destinationPortRange contains '3389'].{name:name, sourceAddressPrefix:sourceAddressPrefix}" --output json
  ```

This automated approach will efficiently ensure compliance with the specified control regarding RDP access to Azure resources.
